title: Mesua stylosa
taxonomy: Mesua stylosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae. It belongs to the genus Mesua and is native to tropical Asia. This species is also known as the horse's hoof tree or the horse's shoe tree due to its distinctive leaf shape. Mesua stylosa is an evergreen tree that grows up to 10 meters tall, with a broad, rounded crown and smooth gray bark. The tree is found in a variety of habitats, including rainforests and dry forests.
uses: Mesua stylosa, also known as the Ceylon ironwood, has been traditionally used in various folk medicines. The plant's bark and leaves are known to possess antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is also used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Furthermore, the plant's extracts are being studied for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's medicinal properties are also being explored for the development of new herbal remedies.
